article_title: Rockefeller's Redesigned CD40 Antibody Eliminates Metastatic Cancers in Phase 1 Trial, Sparking Nearly 200-Patient Expansion

content_preview.title: Rockefeller's Redesigned CD40 Antibody Eliminates Metastatic Cancers in Phase 1 Trial, Sparking Nearly 200-Patient Expansion
content_preview.summary: An Fc-engineered CD40 agonist injected into a single tumor triggered systemic immune responses that eliminated cancer across the body in two patients, with expanded trials now under way at Memorial Sloan Kettering and Duke.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A redesigned cancer immunotherapy drug has produced striking results in a small clinical trial, with two patients achieving complete remission after researchers injected a single tumor and watched cancer disappear across the body. The drug, an Fc-engineered CD40 agonist antibody called 2141-V11, was developed at Rockefeller University under the direction of immunologist Jeffrey V. Ravetch and has now entered expanded testing in nearly 200 patients across multiple cancer types.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent biotech article with clear mechanism explanation and clinical data.
editor_notes.source_verification: ScienceDaily: verified — 2141-V11, Ravetch, 12 patients, 2 complete remissions, intratumoral injection, 10x efficacy, abscopal effect, MSK/Duke expansion, ~200 patients, Cancer Cell publication confirmed. PubMed and PMC provide primary research data.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified. 89-year-old patient age may be from Cancer Cell paper (not in ScienceDaily summary) — minor.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and appropriately cautious about small trial size.
concerns: Patient age (89) not confirmed in ScienceDaily, may be from primary paper
